fields.name: HBITCLKDIV
fields.description: Division ratio for producing half-bit clock The bitfield determines the division ratio (the bitfield value plus one) of a clk divider producing half-bit clock (hbit_clk).

fields.name: IFRGAP
fields.description: Division ratio for producing inter-frame gap timer clock The bitfield determines the division ratio (the bitfield value minus one) of a clk divider producing inter-frame gap timer clock (tInterFrameGap). The division ratio 15 is to apply for Tx clock at the USB PD 2.0 specification nominal value. The division ratios below 15 are to apply for Tx clock below nominal, and the division ratios above 15 for Tx clock above nominal.

fields.name: TRANSWIN
fields.description: Transition window duration The bitfield determines the division ratio (the bitfield value minus one) of a hbit_clk divider producing tTransitionWindow interval. Set a value that produces an interval of 12 to 20 us, taking into account the clk frequency and the HBITCLKDIV[5:0] bitfield setting.

fields.name: PSC_USBPDCLK
fields.description: Pre-scaler division ratio for generating clk The bitfield determines the division ratio of a kernel clock pre-scaler producing peripheral clock (clk). It is recommended to use the pre-scaler so as to set the clk frequency in the range from 6 to 9 MHz.

fields.name: RXORDSETEN
fields.description: Receiver ordered set enable The bitfield determines the types of ordered sets that the receiver must detect. When set/cleared, each bit enables/disables a specific function: 0bxxxxxxxx1: SOP detect enabled 0bxxxxxxx1x: SOP' detect enabled 0bxxxxxx1xx: SOP'' detect enabled 0bxxxxx1xxx: Hard Reset detect enabled 0bxxxx1xxxx: Cable Detect reset enabled 0bxxx1xxxxx: SOP'_Debug enabled 0bxx1xxxxxx: SOP''_Debug enabled 0bx1xxxxxxx: SOP extension#1 enabled 0b1xxxxxxxx: SOP extension#2 enabled

fields.name: TXDMAEN
fields.description: Transmission DMA mode enable When set, the bit enables DMA mode for transmission.

fields.name: RXDMAEN
fields.description: Reception DMA mode enable When set, the bit enables DMA mode for reception.

fields.name: UCPDEN
fields.description: peripheral enable General enable of the peripheral. Upon disabling, the peripheral instantly quits any ongoing activity and all control bits and bitfields default to their reset values. They must be set to their desired values each time the peripheral transits from disabled to enabled state.

fields.name: RXFILTDIS
fields.description: BMC decoder Rx pre-filter enable The sampling clock is that of the receiver (that is, after pre-scaler).

fields.name: RXFILT2N3
fields.description: BMC decoder Rx pre-filter sampling method Number of consistent consecutive samples before confirming a new value.

fields.name: FORCECLK
fields.description: Force ClkReq clock request

fields.name: WUPEN
fields.description: Wakeup from Stop mode enable Setting the bit enables the ASYNC_INT signal.

fields.name: RXAFILTEN
fields.description: Rx analog filter enable Setting the bit enables the Rx analog filter required for optimum Power Delivery reception.

fields.name: TRIM_CC1_RD
fields.description: SW trim value for Rd resistor on the CC1 line

fields.name: TRIM_CC1_RP
fields.description: SW trim value for Rp current sources on the CC1 line

fields.name: TRIM_CC2_RD
fields.description: SW trim value for Rd resistor on the CC2 line

fields.name: TRIM_CC2_RP
fields.description: SW trim value for Rp current sources on the CC2 line

fields.name: TXMODE
fields.description: Type of Tx packet Writing the bitfield triggers the action as follows, depending on the value: Others: invalid From V1.1 of the USB PD specification, there is a counter defined for the duration of the BIST Carrier Mode 2. To quit this mode correctly (after the "tBISTContMode" delay), disable the peripheral (UCPDEN = 0).

fields.name: TXSEND
fields.description: Command to send a Tx packet The bit is cleared by hardware as soon as the packet transmission begins or is discarded.

fields.name: TXHRST
fields.description: Command to send a Tx Hard Reset The bit is cleared by hardware as soon as the message transmission begins or is discarded.

fields.name: RXMODE
fields.description: Receiver mode Determines the mode of the receiver. When the bit is set, RXORDSET behaves normally, RXDR no longer receives bytes yet the CRC checking still proceeds as for a normal message.

fields.name: PHYRXEN
fields.description: USB Power Delivery receiver enable Both CC1 and CC2 receivers are disabled when the bit is cleared. Only the CC receiver selected via the PHYCCSEL bit is enabled when the bit is set.

fields.name: PHYCCSEL
fields.description: CC1/CC2 line selector for USB Power Delivery signaling The selection depends on the cable orientation as discovered at attach.

fields.name: ANASUBMODE
fields.description: Analog PHY sub-mode Refer to TYPEC_VSTATE_CCx for the effect of this bitfield.

fields.name: ANAMODE
fields.description: Analog PHY operating mode The use of CC1 and CC2 depends on CCENABLE. Refer to ANAMODE, ANASUBMODE and link with TYPEC_VSTATE_CCx for the effect of this bitfield in conjunction with ANASUBMODE[1:0].

fields.name: CCENABLE
fields.description: CC line enable This bitfield enables CC1 and CC2 line analog PHYs (pull-ups and pull-downs) according to ANAMODE and ANASUBMODE[1:0] setting. A single line PHY can be enabled when, for example, the other line is driven by VCONN via an external VCONN switch. Enabling both PHYs is the normal usage for sink/source.

fields.name: FRSRXEN
fields.description: FRS event detection enable Setting the bit enables FRS Rx event (FRSEVT) detection on the CC line selected through the PHYCCSEL bit. 0: Disable Clear the bit when the device is attached to an FRS-incapable source/sink.

fields.name: FRSTX
fields.description: FRS Tx signaling enable. Setting the bit enables FRS Tx signaling. The bit is cleared by hardware after a delay respecting the USB Power Delivery specification Revision 3.0.

fields.name: RDCH
fields.description: Rdch condition drive The bit drives Rdch condition on the CC line selected through the PHYCCSEL bit (thus associated with VCONN), by remaining set during the source-only UnattachedWait.SRC state, to respect the Type-C state. Refer to "USB Type-C ECN for Source VCONN Discharge". The CCENABLE[1:0] bitfield must be set accordingly, too.

fields.name: CC1TCDIS
fields.description: CC1 Type-C detector disable The bit disables the Type-C detector on the CC1 line. When enabled, the Type-C detector for CC1 is configured through ANAMODE and ANASUBMODE[1:0].

fields.name: CC2TCDIS
fields.description: CC2 Type-C detector disable The bit disables the Type-C detector on the CC2 line. When enabled, the Type-C detector for CC2 is configured through ANAMODE and ANASUBMODE[1:0].

fields.name: TXISIE
fields.description: TXIS interrupt enable

fields.name: TXMSGDISCIE
fields.description: TXMSGDISC interrupt enable

fields.name: TXMSGSENTIE
fields.description: TXMSGSENT interrupt enable

fields.name: TXMSGABTIE
fields.description: TXMSGABT interrupt enable

fields.name: HRSTDISCIE
fields.description: HRSTDISC interrupt enable

fields.name: HRSTSENTIE
fields.description: HRSTSENT interrupt enable

fields.name: TXUNDIE
fields.description: TXUND interrupt enable

fields.name: RXNEIE
fields.description: RXNE interrupt enable

fields.name: RXORDDETIE
fields.description: RXORDDET interrupt enable

fields.name: RXHRSTDETIE
fields.description: RXHRSTDET interrupt enable

fields.name: RXOVRIE
fields.description: RXOVR interrupt enable

fields.name: RXMSGENDIE
fields.description: RXMSGEND interrupt enable

fields.name: TYPECEVT1IE
fields.description: TYPECEVT1 interrupt enable

fields.name: TYPECEVT2IE
fields.description: TYPECEVT2 interrupt enable

fields.name: FRSEVTIE
fields.description: FRSEVT interrupt enable

fields.name: TXIS
fields.description: Transmit interrupt status The flag indicates that the TXDR register is empty and new data write is required (as the amount of data sent has not reached the payload size defined in the TXPAYSZ bitfield). The flag is cleared with the data write into the TXDR register.

fields.name: TXMSGDISC
fields.description: Message transmission discarded The flag indicates that a message transmission was dropped. The flag is cleared by setting the TXMSGDISCCF bit. Transmission of a message can be dropped if there is a concurrent receive in progress or at excessive noise on the line. After a Tx message is discarded, the flag is only raised when the CC line becomes idle.

fields.name: TXMSGSENT
fields.description: Message transmission completed The flag indicates the completion of packet transmission. It is cleared by setting the TXMSGSENTCF bit. In the event of a message transmission interrupted by a Hard Reset, the flag is not raised.

fields.name: TXMSGABT
fields.description: Transmit message abort The flag indicates that a Tx message is aborted due to a subsequent Hard Reset message send request taking priority during transmit. It is cleared by setting the TXMSGABTCF bit.

fields.name: HRSTDISC
fields.description: Hard Reset discarded The flag indicates that the Hard Reset message is discarded. The flag is cleared by setting the HRSTDISCCF bit.

fields.name: HRSTSENT
fields.description: Hard Reset message sent The flag indicates that the Hard Reset message is sent. The flag is cleared by setting the HRSTSENTCF bit.

fields.name: TXUND
fields.description: Tx data underrun detection The flag indicates that the Tx data register (TXDR) was not written in time for a transmit message to execute normally. It is cleared by setting the TXUNDCF bit.

fields.name: RXNE
fields.description: Receive data register not empty detection The flag indicates that the RXDR register is not empty. It is automatically cleared upon reading RXDR.

fields.name: RXORDDET
fields.description: Rx ordered set (4 K-codes) detection The flag indicates the detection of an ordered set. The relevant information is stored in the RXORDSET[2:0] bitfield of the RX_ORDSET register. It is cleared by setting the RXORDDETCF bit.

fields.name: RXHRSTDET
fields.description: Rx Hard Reset receipt detection The flag indicates the receipt of valid Hard Reset message. It is cleared by setting the RXHRSTDETCF bit.

fields.name: RXOVR
fields.description: Rx data overflow detection The flag indicates Rx data buffer overflow. It is cleared by setting the RXOVRCF bit. The buffer overflow can occur if the received data are not read fast enough.

fields.name: RXMSGEND
fields.description: Rx message received The flag indicates whether a message (except Hard Reset message) has been received, regardless the CRC value. The flag is cleared by setting the RXMSGENDCF bit. The RXERR flag set when the RXMSGEND flag goes high indicates errors in the last-received message.

fields.name: RXERR
fields.description: Receive message error The flag indicates errors of the last Rx message declared (via RXMSGEND), such as incorrect CRC or truncated message (a line becoming static before EOP is met). It is asserted whenever the RXMSGEND flag is set.

fields.name: TYPECEVT1
fields.description: Type-C voltage level event on CC1 line The flag indicates a change of the TYPEC_VSTATE_CC1[1:0] bitfield value, which corresponds to a new Type-C event. It is cleared by setting the TYPECEVT2CF bit.

fields.name: TYPECEVT2
fields.description: Type-C voltage level event on CC2 line The flag indicates a change of the TYPEC_VSTATE_CC2[1:0] bitfield value, which corresponds to a new Type-C event. It is cleared by setting the TYPECEVT2CF bit.

fields.name: TYPEC_VSTATE_CC1
fields.description: The status bitfield indicates the voltage level on the CC1 line in its steady state. The voltage variation on the CC1 line during USB PD messages due to the BMC PHY modulation does not impact the bitfield value.

fields.name: TYPEC_VSTATE_CC2
fields.description: CC2 line voltage level The status bitfield indicates the voltage level on the CC2 line in its steady state. The voltage variation on the CC2 line during USB PD messages due to the BMC PHY modulation does not impact the bitfield value.

fields.name: FRSEVT
fields.description: FRS detection event The flag is cleared by setting the FRSEVTCF bit.

fields.name: TXMSGDISCCF
fields.description: Tx message discard flag (TXMSGDISC) clear Setting the bit clears the TXMSGDISC flag in the SR register.

fields.name: TXMSGSENTCF
fields.description: Tx message send flag (TXMSGSENT) clear Setting the bit clears the TXMSGSENT flag in the SR register.

fields.name: TXMSGABTCF
fields.description: Tx message abort flag (TXMSGABT) clear Setting the bit clears the TXMSGABT flag in the SR register.

fields.name: HRSTDISCCF
fields.description: Hard reset discard flag (HRSTDISC) clear Setting the bit clears the HRSTDISC flag in the SR register.

fields.name: HRSTSENTCF
fields.description: Hard reset send flag (HRSTSENT) clear Setting the bit clears the HRSTSENT flag in the SR register.

fields.name: TXUNDCF
fields.description: Tx underflow flag (TXUND) clear Setting the bit clears the TXUND flag in the SR register.

fields.name: RXORDDETCF
fields.description: Rx ordered set detect flag (RXORDDET) clear Setting the bit clears the RXORDDET flag in the SR register.

fields.name: RXHRSTDETCF
fields.description: Rx Hard Reset detect flag (RXHRSTDET) clear Setting the bit clears the RXHRSTDET flag in the SR register.

fields.name: RXOVRCF
fields.description: Rx overflow flag (RXOVR) clear Setting the bit clears the RXOVR flag in the SR register.

fields.name: RXMSGENDCF
fields.description: Rx message received flag (RXMSGEND) clear Setting the bit clears the RXMSGEND flag in the SR register.

fields.name: TYPECEVT1CF
fields.description: Type-C CC1 event flag (TYPECEVT1) clear Setting the bit clears the TYPECEVT1 flag in the SR register

fields.name: TYPECEVT2CF
fields.description: Type-C CC2 line event flag (TYPECEVT2) clear Setting the bit clears the TYPECEVT2 flag in the SR register

fields.name: FRSEVTCF
fields.description: FRS event flag (FRSEVT) clear Setting the bit clears the FRSEVT flag in the SR register.

fields.name: TXORDSET
fields.description: Ordered set to transmit The bitfield determines a full 20-bit sequence to transmit, consisting of four K-codes, each of five bits, defining the packet to transmit. The bit 0 (bit 0 of K-code1) is the first, the bit 19 (bit 4 of K‑code4) the last.

fields.name: TXPAYSZ
fields.description: Payload size yet to transmit The bitfield is modified by software and by hardware. It contains the number of bytes of a payload (including header but excluding CRC) yet to transmit: each time a data byte is written into the TXDR register, the bitfield value decrements and the TXIS bit is set, except when the bitfield value reaches zero. The enumerated values are standard payload sizes before the start of transmission.

fields.name: TXDATA
fields.description: Data byte to transmit

fields.name: RXORDSET
fields.description: Rx ordered set code detected

fields.name: RXSOP3OF4
fields.description: The bit indicates the number of correct K‑codes. For debug purposes only.

fields.name: RXSOPKINVALID
fields.description: The bitfield is for debug purposes only. Others: Invalid

fields.name: RXPAYSZ
fields.description: Rx payload size received This bitfield contains the number of bytes of a payload (including header but excluding CRC) received: each time a new data byte is received in the RXDR register, the bitfield value increments and the RXMSGEND flag is set (and an interrupt generated if enabled). The bitfield may return a spurious value when a byte reception is ongoing (the RXMSGEND flag is low).

fields.name: RXDATA
fields.description: Data byte received

fields.name: RXSOPX1
fields.description: Ordered set 1 received The bitfield contains a full 20-bit sequence received, consisting of four K‑codes, each of five bits. The bit 0 (bit 0 of K‑code1) is receive first, the bit 19 (bit 4 of K‑code4) last.

fields.name: RXSOPX2
fields.description: Ordered set 2 received The bitfield contains a full 20-bit sequence received, consisting of four K‑codes, each of five bits. The bit 0 (bit 0 of K‑code1) is receive first, the bit 19 (bit 4 of K‑code4) last.

variants.name: Cc1
variants.description: Use CC1 IO for Power Delivery communication

variants.name: Cc2
variants.description: Use CC2 IO for Power Delivery communication

variants.name: Packet
variants.description: Transmission of Tx packet previously defined in other registers
